body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) secured first place on the net foreign list for three consecutive weeks, with a net foreign buy of 536.12 million shares, resulting from foreign buy of 4.79 billion shares and foreign sell of 4.25 billion shares. The company's share price dropped 2.5% to IDR 78 per share, with a transaction volume of 8.20 billion shares worth IDR 639.19 billion, while PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) remained steady with a net foreign buy of 118.09 million shares and a stagnant share price of IDR 91 per share.
title: Goto Gojek Tokopedia records three consecutive weeks as top net foreign buy on Indonesia Stock Exchange.

body: PT Jasa Armada Indonesia Tbk (IPCM) announced an interim dividend distribution of IDR 3.8 per share, totaling IDR 20.05 billion, and its Q3 2024 financial report showed a 10.31% year-on-year (YoY) revenue increase to IDR 946.62 billion and a 1.10% YoY profit increase to IDR 121.10 billion. Despite this, the company's stock price remained stagnant, fluctuating between IDR 270 and IDR 274 per share, and even dropped 4.22% from IDR 284 per share recorded in January 2024.
title: PT Jasa Armada Indonesia's stock price rises after announcing interim dividend distribution despite stagnant trading.

body: Bank IBK Indonesia (IBK) received a capital injection of IDR 1 trillion, resulting in a solid capital position with a 41.33% capital adequacy ratio, and the bank's total assets have grown exponentially by 220% from IDR 6.4 trillion to IDR 20.5 trillion from 2019 to Q3 2024. The bank's total loans and third-party funds also increased by 13.9% and 4.7% year-on-year, respectively, reaching IDR 10.6 trillion and IDR 9.8 trillion in Q3 2024.
title: Bank IBK Indonesia Receives IDR 1 Trillion Capital Injection from IBK Korea, Boosting Liquidity and Assets.

body: According to Bank Indonesia's retail sales survey, the price expectation index is projected to rise to 157.8 in January 2025 before declining to 165.4 in April 2025, driven by seasonal factors such as rainfall and holiday demand normalisation. Retail sales are expected to decrease to 144.7 in January 2025 and 146.5 in April 2025, down from 163.9 in December 2024 and 156.5 in March 2025, respectively.
title: Inflation in Indonesia Expected to Rise in January 2025 Before Declining in April 2025 Due to Seasonal Factors.

body: PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) saw their stock prices decline, with FREN dropping 7.4% to IDR 25, its lowest level this year, and EXCL falling 1.31% to IDR 2,260, despite the companies' merger plan projecting a pre-synergy consolidated value of IDR 104 trillion and revenue of IDR 45.4 trillion. The merger, which is expected to take effect on April 15, 2025, will consolidate the two telecommunication services.
title: XL Axiata and Smartfren Telecom Shares Drop Following Merger Announcement

body: DBS Group Holdings Ltd., Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc., and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc. have submitted separate proposals to acquire PT Bank Pan Indonesia Tbk (Bank Panin), with the bank's stock price rising 19.29% to IDR 1,175 per share, valuing the company at IDR 4.78 trillion. ANZ Group Holdings Ltd. is also considering selling its 38.8% stake in Bank Panin as part of its strategy to divest assets in Asia, while the Gunawan family, which controls 46% of the bank's shares, is open to selling its stake at the right price.
title: Several Asian banks submit proposals to acquire PT Bank Pan Indonesia, with no final decision made yet.

body: Foreign investors bought 4.21 billion shares and sold 4.10 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume surplus of 112.56 million shares, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) ranking first on the top buy list with 1.88 billion shares purchased. On the other hand, PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) topped the sell list with 250.03 million shares sold, followed by PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 357.49 million shares sold.
title: Foreign investors buy and sell Indonesian stocks, with net surplus of 112.56 million shares.

body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) maintained its top position in net foreign transactions, recording 247.11 million shares with a 2.56% increase in share price to IDR 82 per share, despite an 88.46% drop in net foreign transactions from the previous day. PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPi) remained in second place, with a net foreign transaction of 109.72 million shares, and a stable share price of IDR 91 per share.
title: Goto Maintains Top Spot in Net Foreign Transactions Despite 88% Drop

body: PT Bank IBK Indonesia Tbk (IBK) reported a 9.75% increase in net profit to IDR 170.02 billion in Q3-2024, driven by a 15.78% growth in net interest income, with a predicted net profit of IDR 250 billion by the end of 2024. The bank's capital adequacy ratio also improved to 41.33%, and it fulfilled the 7.5% free float share ratio requirement through a share transaction between Industrial Bank Korea and PT KGI Sekuritas Indonesia.
title: Bank IBK Indonesia reports 9.75% increase in net profit for Q3 2024, driven by growth in net interest income and improved capital adequacy ratio.

body: The Indonesian government plans to increase nickel reserves through geological exploration, assigning exploration areas to state-owned and private companies, and auctioning mining zones, aiming to ensure continuity of national nickel supply, a significant source of foreign exchange. Currently, Indonesia holds 42% of the world's total nickel reserves, estimated at 5.3 billion tons, but these reserves are projected to deplete within 25 years, with annual production of nickel ore estimated at 200 million tons.
title: Indonesia Prepares Strategies to Boost Nickel Reserves Amid Depletion Concerns

body: Fore Coffee plans to open 216 outlets across 43 cities in Indonesia, driven by the promising prospects of the coffee industry, with coffee consumption expected to increase by 4.8 million bags in 2024/2025 from 4.45 million bags in 2020/2021, according to a United States Department of Agriculture report. The company's digital platform, which has been downloaded by millions of users since its release in 2018, aims to provide a more personal and interactive experience for customers, capitalizing on the relatively low per capita coffee consumption in Indonesia, currently at 1 kilogram per year.
title: Fore Coffee Expands to 216 Outlets Across 43 Cities in Indonesia Amid Growing Coffee Consumption.

body: PT Delta Dunia Makmur Tbk (DODI) has acquired a majority stake in Atlantic Carbon Group, Inc. for USD 122.4 million, marking its expansion into the US market and providing currency diversification, which is expected to cushion the company from fluctuations in the rupiah and Australian dollar against the US dollar. The acquisition is also expected to contribute to DODI's revenue growth, with non-thermal coal's contribution rising to 28% by the end of the year, aligning with the company's target to reduce thermal coal's contribution to 50% of revenue by 2028.
title: PT Delta Dunia Makmur acquires Atlantic Carbon Group, expanding into US market and diversifying currency mix.

body: PT Dua Putra Utama Makmur Tbk (DPUM) shares surged 33% to IDR 69, the highest price of the trading session, with a total transaction value of IDR 714.77 million, marking a 130% increase from the beginning of the year when the share price was IDR 30 per share. The share price has been strengthening since July 2024, with a peak of IDR 71 last week, before closing at IDR 52 per share on the previous afternoon session.
title: PT Dua Putra Utama Makmur's shares surge 33% to IDR 69 in a single trading session.

body: Djarum Group (no ticker) is acquiring 85% of PT Griya Miesejati, the parent company of Bakmi GM, Indonesia's oldest noodle restaurant chain, for an estimated IDR 2 trillion to IDR 2.4 trillion. The acquisition will expand Djarum Group's presence in the food and beverage sector, where it already has interests through PT Sumber Kopi Prima and PT Savoria Kreasi Rasa.
title: Djarum Group to Acquire 85% Stake in Bakmi GM, Indonesia's Oldest Noodle Restaurant Chain, for Estimated IDR 2-2.4 Trillion.

body: The Indonesian government aims to accelerate economic growth to 8% over the next five years, exceeding projected growth rates of 5.1% in 2024 and 5.2% in 2025, by boosting consumer spending and domestic investment, with retail sales already growing 1.7% year-on-year to IDR 256.5 trillion in Q3 2024. The government plans to lower the incremental capital output ratio (ICOR) to at least 5% to achieve 6% growth, and is developing special economic zones to attract investment, having already attracted IDR 242.5 trillion in investment and created 151,260 jobs.
title: Indonesian Government Unveils Strategies to Boost Economic Growth to 8% Over Next Five Years

body: PT Matahari Department Store Tbk (LPPF) plans to close 13 underperforming outlets this year, with 20 more on watchlist, as part of its effort to reorganize its portfolio, which currently operates 147 outlets. Foreign investors responded by selling 1.03 million shares, resulting in a net sale of 622,200 shares, despite the company's share price still gaining 1.41% to IDR 1,440 per share.
title: Matahari Department Store to Close 13 Underperforming Outlets Amid Foreign Investor Sell-Off

body: PT Tempo Inti Media Tbk (TMPo) saw a 30% jump in stock price, reaching IDR 214 from IDR 164, with a net foreign buy of 192,000 shares, driven by steady growth throughout the day. PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) recorded the highest transaction volume, reaching 838.43 million shares, followed by its subsidiary PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) with 312.92 million shares volume.
title: PT Tempo Inti Media's stock price surges 30% to IDR 214, becoming top gainer of the day.

body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) saw a net foreign buy of 2.14 billion shares, with foreign investors buying 6.44 billion shares and selling 4.30 billion shares, causing its stock price to rise IDR 1 to IDR 78 per share. PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) also experienced a net foreign buy of 161.19 million shares, with its stock price increasing IDR 3 to IDR 91 per share, peaking at IDR 93 per share during the day.
title: Goto Gojek Tokopedia's shares surge with net foreign buy of 2.14 billion shares, closing at IDR 78 per share.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 1.58 billion shares, led by acquisitions of PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 2.14 billion shares and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ASTR) with 161.19 million shares. Meanwhile, shares of PT Exploitasi Energi Indonesia Tbk (EPEN) and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) were sold with net volumes of -718.06 million shares and -130.09 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors purchase 8.28 billion shares in Indonesian market, led by acquisition of digital company PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk.
